steps.ordered: CT Abdomen (CTA ABD W&W/O C & RECONS)

discordance.evidence: ABD- mildly distended, tender to percussion, generalized peritonitis | White Blood Cells: 18.1 K/uL | Neutrophils: 94.7% | Lactate: 2.4 mmol/L (ref 0.5-2.0)

alarm_detect.summary: Generalized peritonitis on exam with markedly elevated WBC (18.1 K/uL, 94.7% neutrophils) and elevated lactate (2.4 mmol/L) without any imaging yet obtained raises concern for a serious intra-abdominal catastrophe that has not yet been characterized.

alarm_resolve.reason: The CT abdomen with and without contrast is the appropriate first-line modality to characterize the source of generalized peritonitis, and the physician's reasoning explicitly targets the discordance between alarming clinical findings (peritonitis, leukocytosis, elevated lactate) and the absence of any imaging by seeking to identify perforation, ischemia, or other intra-abdominal catastrophe.
